summary: Validated new Financial dashboard + Operational expenses feature. Backend: 15/15 new tests in test_financial.py pass + all 22 prior role-based tests still pass (37/37 total). Covers /api/expenses CRUD, /api/financial/closing (default & filtered month), /api/financial/monthly?months=6, role enforcement (salesperson 403 on every new endpoint), and end-to-end closing math (rent 3500 + sold 30000 vehicle with 20000+1000 cost & 500 paid commission produced gross_profit=9000, operational_total=3500, paid_commissions=500, net_profit=5000 — verified per-row contributions and aggregate sums). Frontend (owner): Painel/Visão Geral now shows ONLY 4 count cards (Total/Em Estoque/Reservado/Vendido) — no Investido/Receita/Lucro/Ticket; monthly bar chart shows '1 vendas' (count only). Sidebar 'Financeiro' tab present between Vendedores and Configurações (data-testid=nav-financial). Financial page renders all 4 KPI cards (kpi-gross, kpi-opex, kpi-commissions, kpi-net) with month/year selectors, sold-cars table, expenses table, 6-month chart (FECHAMENTO DO MÊS · 6M). 'Adicionar despesa' modal opens with exp-date/exp-category/exp-description/exp-amount/exp-attachment/exp-submit fields; expense was successfully created via API. Frontend (salesperson): nav-financial NOT visible (correctly hidden). Cleanup: deleted TEST_PW_EXPENSE I created via Playwright; backend test fixtures auto-clean their data; one demo expense 'Aluguel maio 3500' (created earlier by main agent) intentionally left in place. No issues found.
critical_code_review_comments: /api/financial/closing & /api/financial/monthly use string-prefix matching against sold_at (e.g. '2026-05-15T00:00:00') — works because sold_at is consistently saved as ISO string but is fragile; if a vehicle is ever saved with a Z-suffixed or +offset timestamp the range filter could miss it. Consider switching to a real date-typed field or normalising the format in the model.
financial_monthly recomputes per-month totals N times with N+1 DB calls per month — fine for 6 months but consider a single aggregation pipeline if month count grows.

OperationalExpense.date is stored as a string YYYY-MM-DD with no validation — a malformed value (e.g. '2026/05/15') would silently break month filtering. Add a Pydantic validator (regex ^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$) or convert to date type.
OperationalExpense amount has no minimum constraint — negative amounts would be accepted and would inflate net_profit. Consider Field(ge=0).

Financial.jsx and AppShell.jsx rely on browser-default <select> for month/year and <input type=date> for expense date instead of shadcn calendar/select; consistent across app but worth noting per design guidelines.
AppShell.jsx is now 1658 lines (was 1676 — basically unchanged in this iteration); still recommend splitting into per-tab components.
server.py grew to ~1200 lines with the new financial routes; the recommendation to split into modules (auth, vehicles, salespeople, stats, financial, dealership) becomes more pressing.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on owner Financeiro tab + salesperson hidden-tab + Painel-no-money checks

test_credentials: Owner: carlos@intercar.com / senha123 ; Salesperson: joao@intercar.com / senha456 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: test_financial.py creates fixtures (TEST_E2E_RENT expense + TEST_E2E vehicle) in the End-to-End class and tears them down via DELETE /api/expenses/{id} and DELETE /api/vehicles/{id} after the class. CRUD test creates a TEST_RENT expense and deletes it inside the same class. Playwright test created TEST_PW_EXPENSE (777.77, 2026-01-15) which I removed manually post-test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Two test files in /app/backend/tests/: backend_test.py (role-based access matrix) and test_financial.py (financial dashboard + expenses). Both use fixtures that auto-clean their own data, so they are safe to re-run repeatedly. Use BASE_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL — test_financial.py also has a fallback to read /app/frontend/.env if the env var isn't injected by the test runner. The financial-closing aggregation is asserted relationally (sum of rows == aggregate) rather than against fixed values, because there is one demo 'Aluguel maio 3500' expense left in place by the main agent for demo purposes — don't delete it. All new endpoints enforce owner role via require_owner() and are correctly hidden from salesperson UI.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature works end-to-end. No bugs found.
